message:
fix(core): pre-evaluate self-referencing scalar subqueries in DELETE WHERE

When a DELETE's WHERE clause contains an uncorrelated scalar subquery
that reads from the same table being deleted (e.g., DELETE FROM t
WHERE val > (SELECT AVG(val) FROM t)), the VDBE Once opcode only
gates the subquery's OpenRead — the Rewind/AggStep/AggFinal scan
re-executes on every outer row, seeing partially-deleted state.

Add connection-level pre-evaluation: before compiling the DELETE,
detect scalar subqueries that reference the target table, evaluate
them once against the pre-DML state, and substitute the result as
a literal constant. This mirrors the existing correlated-EXISTS
pre-evaluation pattern.

Also adds helper functions:
  - expr_has_self_referencing_scalar_subquery: detects Expr::Subquery
    nodes whose FROM clause references the target table
  - subquery_references_table: checks if a SELECT reads from a given
    table (including JOIN sources)
  - inline_self_referencing_scalar_subqueries: recursively evaluates
    and substitutes matching subqueries with literal constants

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
